source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 序言; 序曲; 事件的开端 an introduction to something more substantial: 7 sensed that this was the overture to an argument. 我察觉到这是引向一场争吵的序曲

headline.core_en: an opening piece, action, or proposal that begins something larger

familiar_bridge.summary: opening 是“开头”，overture 更正式：可以是音乐的序曲，也可以是外交、合作或关系中的开场动作。

core.main: overture 的共同点是“正式开始前的开场”：音乐里是序曲，社交或政治语境中是试探性的提议或姿态。

senses.label: 音乐序曲
senses.text: 歌剧或大型作品开头的器乐部分。

senses.label: 试探性提议或姿态
senses.text: 为了开启谈判、合作或关系而做出的初步行动。

usage_groups.group_title: 音乐或作品开头的序曲
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 为后面更大的作品或事件做铺垫。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 音乐、戏剧、文学比喻。

examples.sentence: The overture introduced the themes that would return throughout the opera.
examples.translation: 这首序曲引出了整部歌剧中会反复出现的主题。
examples.teaching_note: 音乐义强调作品开头的铺垫。

usage_groups.group_title: 谈判或合作前的试探性提议
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 还不是正式协议，而是开启接触的第一步。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 外交、商业、政治、社交关系。

examples.sentence: The government made cautious overtures to its former rivals.
examples.translation: 政府向过去的对手作出了谨慎的接触姿态。
examples.teaching_note: make overtures 表示主动试探接触。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech served as the opening of the conference.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这场演讲作为会议的开场。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: opening 很普通；overture 更正式，常有铺垫或试探意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The small protest was a prelude to wider unrest.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场小规模抗议是更大范围动荡的前奏。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prelude 与 overture 接近，但更强调后来事件的前兆。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The board rejected the proposal for a merger.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 董事会拒绝了合并提议。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: proposal 是明确提案；overture 往往更试探、更初步。

antonyms.example_sentence: The finale brought the audience to its feet.
antonyms.example_translation: 终曲让观众起立喝彩。
antonyms.difference_note: finale 与 overture 的开场方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The rejection of the peace offer ended the talks.
antonyms.example_translation: 和平提议被拒绝后，谈判结束了。
antonyms.difference_note: 在提议语境中，rejection 是 overture 的失败结果。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The policy faced overt opposition.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这项政策遭到公开反对。
confusable_words.difference_note: overt 形近但表示公开明显，不是 overture 的名词形式。

word_family.note: 复数形式常用于外交或合作语境。

related_words.note: 帮助理解“开场铺垫”的逻辑。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: overture 已覆盖音乐序曲和试探性提议两大核心义，避免只讲 introduction。
issues_found: 旧释义遗漏 diplomatic overtures 这类重要用法
revision_actions: 补充 make overtures 和 peace/diplomatic overtures
区分 overt 形近词

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 创新的; 先驱的 introducing ideas and methods that have never been used before: his pioneering work on consciousness 他对意识的创新研究

headline.core_en: introducing new ideas, methods, or practices for the first time

familiar_bridge.summary: pioneer 是“先驱者”，pioneering 就是走在前面、开出一条新路的。

core.main: pioneering 强调某项工作或方法在当时很新，后来可能为整个领域打开方向。

senses.label: 开创性的；先驱性的
senses.text: 形容研究、方法、技术或人物率先做出新尝试。

usage_groups.group_title: 研究、方法或技术开创新方向
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是普通创新，而是早期率先尝试并影响后来发展。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 科学研究、医学、技术、社会改革、艺术实践。

examples.sentence: Her pioneering research changed how scientists understood memory.
examples.translation: 她的开创性研究改变了科学家对记忆的理解。
examples.teaching_note: pioneering research 强调它为后来的研究打开方向。

examples.sentence: The hospital adopted a pioneering technique for treating rare tumors.
examples.translation: 这家医院采用了一种治疗罕见肿瘤的开创性技术。
examples.teaching_note: pioneering technique 常用于医学或科技语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team tested a new method.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 团队测试了一种新方法。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: new 只是新；pioneering 强调率先开创。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company is known for innovative design.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这家公司以创新设计闻名。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: innovative 强调有新意；pioneering 更强调先驱地位。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study made a groundbreaking discovery.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项研究取得了突破性发现。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: groundbreaking 语气更强，强调重大突破。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor's trailblazing work reshaped the field.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位教授的开创性工作重塑了该领域。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: trailblazing 和 pioneering 很近，画面是开路。

antonyms.example_sentence: The proposal relied on conventional methods.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个方案依赖常规方法。
antonyms.difference_note: conventional 与 pioneering 的开创新路相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The essay offered an original interpretation.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这篇文章提出了原创性解释。
confusable_words.difference_note: original 强调不是抄袭或有独创性；pioneering 强调先驱影响。

related_words.note: 和 pioneer 的画面相近。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: pioneering 已围绕开创新方向展开，避免只说 innovative。

需要区分 pioneering 和 merely new

加入 pioneer/trailblazer 迁移
Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t， 使和人迷; 迷住 attract and hold the interest and attention of; charm: He was captivated with her charm. 他被她的魅力迷住了 。 | be captivated by a story RR BARE

headline.core_en: to attract and hold someone's attention or interest completely

familiar_bridge.summary: capture 是“抓住”，captivate 像是“抓住注意力和心”，所以是让人被深深吸引。

core.main: captivate 强调不只是吸引一下，而是让听众、读者或观众被抓住注意力，愿意继续看、听或想。

senses.label: 迷住；深深吸引
senses.text: 形容故事、音乐、演讲、人物魅力或景象让人无法移开注意。

usage_groups.group_title: 作品、表演或人物魅力吸引住人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 注意力被牢牢抓住，通常带愉悦或赞赏。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 小说、电影、演讲、音乐、风景、人物魅力。

examples.sentence: The speaker captivated the audience with a simple but powerful story.
examples.translation: 演讲者用一个简单却有力量的故事吸引住了观众。
examples.teaching_note: captivate the audience 是非常自然的搭配。

examples.sentence: Readers were captivated by the novel's quiet but unsettling atmosphere.
examples.translation: 读者被这部小说安静却令人不安的氛围深深吸引。
examples.teaching_note: be captivated by 强调被某事吸引住。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The exhibit attracted thousands of visitors.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个展览吸引了数千名游客。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: attract 普通；captivate 更强调持续抓住注意力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The mystery fascinated generations of scholars.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个谜团让几代学者着迷。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fascinate 常带好奇或智性吸引。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The performance enthralled the entire audience.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场表演让全场观众入迷。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: enthrall 比 captivate 更强、更文学。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The dancer's movements mesmerized the crowd.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 舞者的动作让人群看得入迷。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mesmerize 带近乎催眠的吸引感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The lecture bored most of the students.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场讲座让大多数学生感到无聊。
antonyms.difference_note: bore 与 captivate 的吸引力相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The harsh tone repelled potential supporters.
antonyms.example_translation: 这种严厉语气让潜在支持者反感。
antonyms.difference_note: repel 强调让人远离。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The photo captured the mood of the protest.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这张照片捕捉到了抗议现场的气氛。
confusable_words.difference_note: capture 可以是实体抓住或记录；captivate 主要抓住注意力和兴趣。

extension.root_affix: capt- 有“抓住”的感觉；captivate 是把注意力或情感“抓住”。

word_family.note: 形容具有吸引力的人或事物。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: captivate 已从 capture 的抓住画面自然引入，并覆盖 audience/readers/story 语境。

需要补充 captivate 与 capture 区别

重写 usage_tree

需要补充 captivate 与 capture 区别

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 误导性的; 骗人的; 迷惑人的 giving the wrong idea or impression and making you believe something that is not true: misleading advertising 骗人的广告

headline.core_cn: 误导性的；容易使人误解的
headline.core_en: giving a wrong idea or impression

familiar_bridge.summary: lead 是“带领”，mislead 是“带错方向”；misleading 就是让人形成错误印象。

core.main: misleading 强调信息表面上看似有用或真实，但会把判断带偏，让人误解事实。

senses.label: 误导性的
senses.text: 形容数字、广告、说法、标题或证据让人得出错误结论。

usage_groups.group_title: 信息、数字或表述让人误判
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 问题不一定是完全虚假，而是会制造错误印象。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 统计数据、广告、标题、证据、说法、图表。

examples.sentence: The chart was misleading because it omitted the years when prices fell.
examples.translation: 这张图表具有误导性，因为它省略了价格下降的年份。
examples.teaching_note: misleading 常用于数据或图表让人误判。

examples.sentence: The advertisement made misleading claims about the drug's benefits.
examples.translation: 这则广告对药物疗效作出了误导性宣称。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 misleading 表示广告说法会把人带向错误判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article contained several false claims.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章包含几处不实说法。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: false 是直接错误；misleading 可以是真中带偏。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The packaging was deceptive because it made the product look larger.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 包装具有欺骗性，因为它让产品看起来更大。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deceptive 更强调有欺骗效果，甚至可能有意。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument sounded plausible but was specious.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个论证听起来合理，但其实似是而非。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: specious 常形容论证表面合理、实则有问题。

antonyms.example_sentence: The map gives an accurate picture of the region.
antonyms.example_translation: 这张地图准确呈现了该地区。
antonyms.difference_note: accurate 与 misleading 的错误印象相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: A truthful account should include the uncomfortable details.
antonyms.example_translation: 真实叙述应包含那些令人不适的细节。
antonyms.difference_note: truthful 强调诚实和真实。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The instructions were confusing even though they were not false.
confusable_words.example_translation: 说明书令人困惑，虽然并不虚假。
confusable_words.difference_note: confusing 是难懂；misleading 是把人带向错误判断。

extension.root_affix: mis- 表示错误；lead 表示引导。misleading 的底层画面是“把人引向错误方向”。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: misleading 已明确区分 false、confusing 和 deceptive，并补足数据/广告/图表语境。

需要强调 misleading 不等于完全 false
revision_actions: 重写核心解释

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 肯定地说; 坚称 state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully: He asserted his ideas loudly and clearly. 他大声明确地说出自己的想法 。 | He asserted his innocence. 他坚称自己无罪 。 3044 maintain

headline.core_en: to state something confidently, or to make a right or authority felt

familiar_bridge.summary: say 是“说”，assert 是非常肯定地说；也可以表示把权利、权威或自我立场明确表现出来。

core.main: assert 的重点是“坚定地提出或体现出来”：可以是断言一个观点，也可以是维护权利、权威或自我。

senses.label: 断言；坚称
senses.text: 自信有力地说某事为真。

senses.label: 维护；显示权利或权威
senses.text: 让某种权利、控制力或存在感被看见。

usage_groups.group_title: 坚定提出观点或事实判断
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说话者语气肯定，像是在明确立场。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 论证、学术写作、政治声明、法律陈述。

examples.sentence: The author asserts that economic growth alone cannot solve social inequality.
examples.translation: 作者断言，仅靠经济增长无法解决社会不平等。
examples.teaching_note: assert that 常用于文章观点或论证。

usage_groups.group_title: 维护权利、权威或自我立场
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让权利、控制力或自我存在感发挥出来。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 权利、权威、控制、个人表达。

examples.sentence: The community asserted its right to participate in decisions about the land.
examples.translation: 这个社区维护了自己参与土地决策的权利。
examples.teaching_note: assert one's rights 表示主动维护权利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report states that the policy failed.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 报告说明这项政策失败了。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: state 中性陈述；assert 更坚定有力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company claimed that the product was safe.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 公司声称该产品是安全的。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: claim 可带未经证实的意味；assert 更强调说话者自信。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ruling affirmed the citizens' rights.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项裁决确认了公民权利。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: affirm 更强调确认或支持。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scholar maintained that the evidence was incomplete.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位学者坚持认为证据并不完整。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: maintain 强调持续坚持某观点。

antonyms.example_sentence: The official denied any involvement in the scandal.
antonyms.example_translation: 这名官员否认参与丑闻。
antonyms.difference_note: deny 与 assert a claim 的方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The journal retracted the article after errors were found.
antonyms.example_translation: 发现错误后，期刊撤回了这篇文章。
antonyms.difference_note: retract 是收回已说出的话或已发表内容。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The doctor assured the patient that the pain was normal.
confusable_words.example_translation: 医生向病人保证这种疼痛是正常的。
confusable_words.difference_note: assure 后面常接人；assert 强调提出观点或权利。

word_family.note: 名词形式，常用于沟通能力。

related_words.note: 常和 assert 一起辨析。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: assert 已覆盖断言和维护权利两义，避免只按 say 处理。

需要处理 assert rights/authority
revision_actions: 补充两组 usage
补齐 assertion/assertive 词族
关系区区分 state、claim、affirm、maintain

需要处理 assert rights/authority

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 坚定的; #29 (a) disposed to or characterized by bold or confident statements and behavior: speak in an assertive tone 以极为自信的口吻说话

headline.core_cn: 坚定自信的；敢于表达的
headline.core_en: confident in expressing opinions, needs, or rights without being passive

familiar_bridge.summary: assert 是“明确说出、维护”，assertive 形容一个人敢表达、敢维护自己的立场。

core.main: assertive 不是 rude，也不一定 aggressive；它强调清楚、自信地表达想法、需求或边界。

senses.label: 坚定自信的；敢表达的
senses.text: 形容人在沟通和行动中不被动，能清楚表达自己。

usage_groups.group_title: 表达立场或需求时坚定自信
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不退缩、不含糊，但也不必带攻击性。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 沟通、领导力、课堂讨论、工作会议、人际关系。

examples.sentence: She became more assertive in meetings and began to challenge weak arguments.
examples.translation: 她在会议中变得更敢表达，也开始质疑薄弱的论证。
examples.teaching_note: assertive in meetings 强调积极表达立场。

examples.sentence: An assertive tone can be firm without being hostile.
examples.translation: 坚定自信的语气可以很明确，而不必充满敌意。
examples.teaching_note: 这句帮助区分 assertive 和 aggressive。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speaker sounded confident even under pressure.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 演讲者即使在压力下也显得自信。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confident 更泛；assertive 特别强调表达和维护立场。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her self-assured manner reassured the team.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她自信从容的态度让团队安心。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: self-assured 偏稳定自信；assertive 偏主动表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He made a forceful argument for reform.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他为改革提出了有力论证。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: forceful 强调表达有力量，可能比 assertive 更强。

antonyms.example_sentence: A passive response allowed the problem to continue.
antonyms.example_translation: 被动回应让问题继续存在。
antonyms.difference_note: passive 与 assertive 的主动表达相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The timid student rarely asked questions.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个胆怯的学生很少提问。
antonyms.difference_note: timid 强调缺乏胆量。

confusable_words.meaning_cn: 咄咄逼人的；有攻击性的
confusable_words.example_sentence: His aggressive tone alienated potential allies.
confusable_words.example_translation: 他咄咄逼人的语气疏远了潜在盟友。
confusable_words.difference_note: aggressive 可能侵犯他人；assertive 是清楚表达自己。

extension.root_affix: 来自 assert：明确提出或维护。assertive 描述这种表达方式形成的性格或语气。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: assertive 已清楚区分 confident、forceful、aggressive，学生可直接理解。

需要避免把 assertive 简单等同 aggressive
revision_actions: 重写核心义和 usage_tree
补充 assert/assertion/assertiveness 词族

需要避免把 assertive 简单等同 aggressive

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 刺激物; 激励物 something that increases activity, interest, or enthusiasm in a specified field: Hope is a stimulant. 希望激励人们向前 。 | The proposals will be promoted as stimulants for sagging business. 这些建议将被推行 ， 以刺激日益萎缩的商业

headline.core_cn: 刺激物；兴奋剂；激励因素
headline.core_en: a substance or factor that increases activity, energy, interest, or growth

familiar_bridge.summary: stimulate 是“刺激、促进”，stimulant 就是能让身体、经济、兴趣或活动变得更活跃的东西。

core.main: stimulant 可以是咖啡因这类让身体兴奋的物质，也可以是让经济、思想或兴趣活跃起来的因素。

senses.label: 生理刺激物；兴奋剂
senses.text: 让身体或神经系统更活跃的物质。

senses.label: 激励因素；促进因素
senses.text: 让某种活动、兴趣或增长增强的原因。

usage_groups.group_title: 让身体或神经系统更活跃的物质
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常指咖啡因、药物或兴奋剂。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 医学、健康、药物、运动禁药讨论。

examples.sentence: Caffeine is a mild stimulant that can make people feel more alert.
examples.translation: 咖啡因是一种温和刺激物，能让人感觉更清醒。
examples.teaching_note: stimulant 在医学和日常语境中常指物质。

usage_groups.group_title: 促使活动、兴趣或增长增强的因素
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不一定是物质，也可以是政策、竞争、需求或新思想。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 经济、教育、文化、科研、市场。

examples.sentence: Competition can be a stimulant to innovation when firms must improve to survive.
examples.translation: 当企业必须改进才能生存时，竞争可以成为创新的刺激因素。
examples.teaching_note: a stimulant to innovation/growth 表示促进某事活跃起来的因素。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The tax cut acted as a stimulus to consumer spending.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 减税对消费支出起到了刺激作用。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stimulus 更泛；stimulant 可指具体物质或促进因素。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The award provided an incentive for further research.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个奖项为进一步研究提供了激励。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incentive 强调让人愿意行动的诱因。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis became a catalyst for reform.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场危机成为改革的催化剂。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: catalyst 强调触发或加速变化。

antonyms.example_sentence: Alcohol can act as a depressant on the nervous system.
antonyms.example_translation: 酒精会对神经系统起抑制作用。
antonyms.difference_note: depressant 在生理意义上与 stimulant 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The doctor prescribed a mild sedative.
antonyms.example_translation: 医生开了一种温和镇静剂。
antonyms.difference_note: sedative 让身体或情绪平静下来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The brain responds differently to visual stimulation.
confusable_words.example_translation: 大脑对视觉刺激的反应不同。
confusable_words.difference_note: stimulation 是过程或作用；stimulant 是产生作用的物质或因素。

extension.root_affix: stim- 与“刺激、推动”有关；stimulant 是能产生刺激作用的东西。

word_family.note: 形容让人兴奋或思考的事物。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: stimulant 已区分生理刺激物和抽象促进因素，关系与派生网络完整。

需要补充 depressant/sedative 反向词
revision_actions: 重写两组 usage
补充 stimulate/stimulation/stimulating 词族
加入 economic stimulant 和 stimulant to growth 结构

需要补充 depressant/sedative 反向词

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: 343444 X adj. 邻人兴奋的; 吸引人的 encouraging or arousing interest or enthusiasm: a stimulating conversation 吸引人的对话 i 444] exhilarating, provocative

headline.core_cn: 有启发性的；令人兴奋的；能激发兴趣的
headline.core_en: interesting or exciting enough to make people think, feel energetic, or become more active

familiar_bridge.summary: stimulate 是“刺激、激发”，stimulating 形容一件事能把人的兴趣、思考或活力调动起来。

core.main: stimulating 不只是 fun，而是让人精神更活跃：一场对话、一本书、一个环境或一项活动都可以 stimulating。

senses.label: 激发兴趣或思考
senses.text: 形容谈话、书、课程或讨论让人觉得有启发、有活力。

senses.label: 使身体或精神兴奋
senses.text: 形容活动、饮品或环境带来能量。

usage_groups.group_title: 谈话、书或课程让人有启发
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让人愿意继续思考或讨论。

examples.sentence: The seminar was stimulating because it forced students to question familiar assumptions.
examples.translation: 这场研讨课很有启发性，因为它迫使学生质疑熟悉的假设。
examples.teaching_note: stimulating 在学习语境中强调激发思考。

usage_groups.group_title: 活动或环境让人更有活力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让身体、情绪或注意力更活跃。
usage_groups.when_to_use: exercise, environment, experience, drink。

examples.sentence: A stimulating environment can encourage children to explore and ask questions.
examples.translation: 一个有激发性的环境能鼓励孩子探索和提问。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调环境促进主动活动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The book was interesting enough to keep me reading.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这本书足够有趣，让我一直读下去。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interesting 偏吸引注意；stimulating 更强调激发思考或活力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor gave an engaging lecture on urban history.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 教授做了一场吸引人的城市史讲座。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: engaging 强调让人投入；stimulating 强调激发反应。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay was thought-provoking without being difficult.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章发人深省，但并不难懂。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: thought-provoking 更专门指激发思考。

antonyms.example_sentence: The meeting was dull and produced no new ideas.
antonyms.example_translation: 会议很乏味，也没有产生新想法。
antonyms.difference_note: dull 与 stimulating 的活力和启发性相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The lecture was boring because it repeated facts everyone knew.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场讲座很无聊，因为它重复了大家都知道的事实。
antonyms.difference_note: boring 是普通反义词。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The article made a provocative claim about democracy.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这篇文章提出了一个关于民主的挑衅性观点。
confusable_words.difference_note: provocative 可激发争议；stimulating 不一定有挑衅性。

extension.root_affix: 来自 stimulate：刺激、激发。stimulating 是它的形容词形式。

word_family.note: 能产生刺激作用的东西。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: stimulating 已按智性启发和活力激发两层组织，学生可见文案自然。

需要区分 stimulating 和 provocative

补齐 stimulate/stimulation/stimulant 词族

需要区分 stimulating 和 provocative

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt. FE; 安抚;使息怒 to make sb. feel less angry about sth.: placate the opposition 安抚反对派近义词 appease 9 implacable /im'plekobl/ adj. 无法和解的; 无法平息的 unable to be placated: an implacable enemy 死敌 | the implacable hatred that workers feel for their employers 工人对雇主无法消解的仇恨还双启 unyielding

headline.core_en: to make someone less angry or hostile

familiar_bridge.summary: calm 是“让平静”，placate 更具体：面对已经生气或不满的人，做点事让对方消气。

core.main: placate 常用于冲突、投诉、抗议或政治压力中，表示通过解释、让步或补偿来安抚不满。

senses.label: 安抚不满或愤怒的人
senses.text: 让某人从愤怒、敌意或不满中缓和下来。

usage_groups.group_title: 安抚愤怒或不满的人群
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 对象通常已经不满，需要通过行动或话语缓和情绪。

examples.sentence: The mayor offered a public apology to placate angry residents.
examples.translation: 市长公开道歉，以安抚愤怒的居民。
examples.teaching_note: placate 常带“通过某种姿态让对方消气”的感觉。

usage_groups.group_title: 通过让步或解释降低压力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是解决所有问题，而是暂时缓和反对或紧张。
usage_groups.when_to_use: politics, business, public relations。

examples.sentence: The company lowered its fees in an attempt to placate frustrated users.
examples.translation: 公司降低费用，试图安抚感到沮丧的用户。
examples.teaching_note: an attempt to placate 表示缓和不满的措施。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher calmed the class before the test began.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 考试开始前，老师让全班安静下来。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: calm 很普通；placate 更强调平息不满或怒气。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy was designed to appease conservative critics.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项政策旨在安抚保守派批评者。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: appease 可带负面色彩，像通过让步息事宁人。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The explanation did little to mollify the angry audience.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个解释几乎没有平息愤怒观众的情绪。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mollify 更书面，和 placate 很近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The remark provoked an immediate backlash.
antonyms.example_translation: 这句话立刻激起反弹。
antonyms.difference_note: provoke 与 placate 的方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The delay only aggravated the passengers.
antonyms.example_translation: 延误只让乘客更加恼火。
antonyms.difference_note: aggravate 让不满更严重。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The army was sent to pacify the region.
confusable_words.example_translation: 军队被派去平定该地区。
confusable_words.difference_note: pacify 可用于地区或局势；placate 更常接人或群体情绪。

extension.root_affix: plac- 与“平静、使满意”的感觉相关；placate 是使人怒气下降。

word_family.note: 形容话语或姿态意在平息怒气。

related_words.note: 常用于政治或批评语境。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: placate 已围绕安抚怒气组织，近反义和例句均服务同一语义场。

需要补充政治和公众情绪语境

补充 appease/mollify/pacify 区分
加入 placation/placatory 词族

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (问题等的 ) 方面 a particular aspect or feature of something: transform all facets of life 7 变生活的各个方面 | Selfishness was a facet of his character that we seldom saw. 自私是他性格上我们很少注意到的一面

headline.core_en: one aspect or part of a larger subject, problem, or personality

familiar_bridge.summary: face 是“面”，facet 像一个多面体的一个面；用在抽象事物上，就是一个问题或性格的某一面。

core.main: facet 强调一个复杂整体中的某个侧面，因此常和 problem、issue、personality、culture 这类词搭配。

senses.label: 复杂事物的一个方面
senses.text: 指问题、人物、制度或文化中的某个侧面。

usage_groups.group_title: 问题、主题或人格的某一面
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把复杂整体拆成若干方面来看。

examples.sentence: The report examines only one facet of the problem: access to clean water.
examples.translation: 这份报告只考察了这个问题的一个方面：清洁用水的获取。
examples.teaching_note: facet 常用于说明复杂问题的某个侧面。

usage_groups.group_title: 宝石或物体的切面
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 实体意义上，一个被切割出来的平面。
usage_groups.when_to_use: gemstones, crystals, surfaces。

examples.sentence: Each facet of the diamond reflected light differently.
examples.translation: 钻石的每一个切面都以不同方式反射光线。
examples.teaching_note: 这个实体义帮助理解抽象义的“侧面”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay focuses on one aspect of urban poverty.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章关注城市贫困的一个方面。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: aspect 最普通；facet 更有“复杂整体的一个面”的画面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The debate has a moral dimension as well as an economic one.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这场辩论既有经济层面，也有道德层面。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dimension 强调分析维度；facet 强调侧面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: One feature of the system is its flexibility.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个系统的一个特点是灵活性。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: feature 偏特点；facet 偏复杂整体的一面。

antonyms.example_sentence: The whole cannot be understood by studying one part alone.
antonyms.example_translation: 只研究一个部分无法理解整体。
antonyms.difference_note: whole 与 facet 的部分视角相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The faucet leaked all night.
confusable_words.example_translation: 水龙头漏了一整夜。
confusable_words.difference_note: faucet 拼写接近但意义完全不同。

extension.root_affix: facet 本身可记作“一个面”；它从实体切面延伸到抽象侧面。

word_family.note: 可形容宝石，也可比喻复杂。
非常有用的迁移词，表示一个人或问题有多个层面。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: facet 已从实体切面到抽象侧面建立清晰学习路径。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (货物 、 服务 、 消息等的 ) 供应者; 提供者 one that purveys: a purveyor of gossip 散播流言翡语的人 | a purveyor of joy and laughter 带来欢笑的人

headline.core_en: a person or organization that supplies goods, services, or ideas

familiar_bridge.summary: provide 是“提供”，purveyor 更正式，常指专门提供某类商品、服务、消息或观念的人或机构。

core.main: purveyor 不只用于卖东西，也可用于“传播某类思想、消息或风格”的人，常带一点评价色彩。

senses.label: 商品、服务或观念的提供者
senses.text: 指持续提供某类东西的人、公司或机构。

usage_groups.group_title: 提供商品或服务的人或机构
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某人或机构以某类东西为供应内容。

examples.sentence: The company presents itself as a purveyor of high-quality organic food.
examples.translation: 这家公司把自己定位为高质量有机食品的供应者。
examples.teaching_note: a purveyor of 后面接它提供的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 传播消息、观念或风格的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 提供的不一定是实物，也可以是观点、流言或文化风格。

examples.sentence: The website became a purveyor of misinformation during the election.
examples.translation: 这个网站在选举期间成了错误信息的传播者。
examples.teaching_note: purveyor of misinformation 带负面评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The school became a provider of free meals for local children.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这所学校成了当地儿童免费餐食的提供者。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: provider 普通；purveyor 更正式，也常带评价。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The factory changed its main supplier of steel.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 工厂更换了主要钢材供应商。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: supplier 更偏商业供应链。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Several vendors sold food outside the stadium.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 几名商贩在体育场外卖食物。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vendor 更偏销售者或摊贩。

antonyms.example_sentence: The recipient of the grant must submit a final report.
antonyms.example_translation: 资助接收者必须提交最终报告。
antonyms.difference_note: recipient 是接收者，与 purveyor 的提供者方向相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The surveyor measured the boundaries of the property.
confusable_words.example_translation: 测量员测量了这块地产的边界。
confusable_words.difference_note: surveyor 形近但和供应无关。

extension.root_affix: purvey 是“供应、提供”，purveyor 是执行这种提供的人或机构。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 策重的 large or heavy and therefore difficult to carry or use; unwieldy: cumbersome boots KEHKAM 35044) unwieldy

headline.core_en: large, awkward, or complicated enough to be difficult to use or manage

familiar_bridge.summary: cumbersome 和 unwieldy 很近：东西可能太笨重，流程也可能太繁琐，所以不好用。

core.main: cumbersome 既能形容实体设备笨重，也能形容程序、制度或规则复杂拖沓。

senses.label: 笨重难用；繁琐难处理
senses.text: 因为体积、重量或复杂度而不方便。

usage_groups.group_title: 物体或设备笨重难操作
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 体积、重量或设计让使用变麻烦。
usage_groups.when_to_use: equipment, clothing, tools, machinery。

examples.sentence: The old diving suit was too cumbersome for quick movement underwater.
examples.translation: 旧式潜水服太笨重，水下无法快速移动。
examples.teaching_note: 实体义强调笨重难移动。

usage_groups.group_title: 流程、制度或规则繁琐难用
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是重，而是步骤复杂、效率低。
usage_groups.when_to_use: procedures, rules, systems, bureaucracy。

examples.sentence: The application process was so cumbersome that many families gave up halfway.
examples.translation: 申请流程太繁琐，许多家庭中途放弃。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象义常批评制度或流程效率低。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The handle made the tool awkward to use.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个把手让工具用起来不方便。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: awkward 更泛；cumbersome 常因重量、体积或复杂度造成不便。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The camera was too unwieldy for fieldwork.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这台相机太笨重，不适合实地工作。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unwieldy 和 cumbersome 很近，强调不好操控。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new reporting rules placed onerous demands on small firms.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 新的报告规定给小公司带来沉重负担。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: onerous 更强调负担沉重，不一定笨重。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new system is streamlined and easy to use.
antonyms.example_translation: 新系统简化高效，易于使用。
antonyms.difference_note: streamlined 与 cumbersome 的繁琐相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The compact device fits easily into a bag.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个紧凑设备很容易放进包里。
antonyms.difference_note: compact 与实体笨重相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Debt encumbered the company for years.
confusable_words.example_translation: 债务多年拖累这家公司。
confusable_words.difference_note: encumber 是动词；cumbersome 是形容词。

extension.root_affix: cumber 有“负担、妨碍”的感觉；cumbersome 就是带来负担、难以处理。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: cumbersome 已覆盖笨重和繁琐两大场景，派生层未混入普通搭配。

需要补全抽象流程义

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Wilt; 阻碍 restrict or burden (someone or something) in such a way that free action or movement is difficult: The irrigation project was encumbered by lack of funds. 灌溉工程因经费缺乏而停顿 。 94 unencumbered /,anin'kambod/ adj. 不受妨碍的; 没有阻碍的 not having any burden or impediment: a man unencumbered by material considerations 不为物质考虑烦扰的人

headline.core_en: to burden or restrict someone or something so that movement or action becomes difficult

familiar_bridge.summary: cumbersome 是“笨重繁琐的”，encumber 是动词：给某人或某物加上负担，让它动不了、做不顺。

core.main: encumber 常用于债务、规则、责任、装备或法律限制拖累行动。

senses.label: 拖累；阻碍自由行动
senses.text: 让人、组织、财产或行动因为负担而难以前进。

usage_groups.group_title: 债务、规则或责任拖累行动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 负担太重，让行动不自由或效率下降。
usage_groups.when_to_use: debt, regulations, obligations, bureaucracy。

examples.sentence: The company was encumbered by debt and could not invest in new technology.
examples.translation: 这家公司被债务拖累，无法投资新技术。
examples.teaching_note: be encumbered by debt 是很自然的结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 装备或障碍让移动变困难
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 身体或物体被负担拖住，移动不便。
usage_groups.when_to_use: equipment, luggage, armor, obstacles。

examples.sentence: The hikers were encumbered with heavy packs on the steep trail.
examples.translation: 徒步者背着沉重背包，在陡峭小路上行动不便。
examples.teaching_note: 实体义强调被负重拖住。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Extra paperwork burdened the staff.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 额外文书工作加重了员工负担。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: burden 普通；encumber 更强调负担造成行动受限。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Poor roads hampered the rescue effort.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 糟糕的道路妨碍了救援工作。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: hamper 强调行动被阻碍。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lack of data impeded further analysis.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 数据不足阻碍了进一步分析。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impede 更正式，强调进展变慢。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform freed businesses from outdated restrictions.
antonyms.example_translation: 改革让企业摆脱了过时限制。
antonyms.difference_note: free 与 encumber 的拖累限制相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new system unburdened teachers of repetitive tasks.
antonyms.example_translation: 新系统让教师摆脱了重复性任务。
antonyms.difference_note: unburden 直接表示卸负担。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The old procedure was cumbersome and slow.
confusable_words.example_translation: 旧流程繁琐而缓慢。
confusable_words.difference_note: cumbersome 是形容词；encumber 是让某事变得受限的动词。

extension.root_affix: en- 可表示“使进入某种状态”；cumber 表示负担或妨碍。encumber 就是“使负担缠身”。

word_family.note: 常见形容词和过去分词。
名词形式，法律和金融语境也常见。
同一负担意象的形容词。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: encumber 已明确为动词，语义场和派生网络干净。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 区残的; 残忍的 deliberately cruel or violent: a vicious assault 残忍的攻击

headline.core_cn: 恶毒的；残忍的；猛烈有害的
headline.core_en: deliberately cruel, violent, or dangerously harmful

familiar_bridge.summary: vicious 比 bad 强得多，常带“恶意、残忍、伤害性强”的感觉。

core.main: vicious 可形容人、攻击、话语，也可形容恶性循环：问题互相加强，越来越糟。

senses.label: 残忍或恶毒的
senses.text: 形容攻击、行为、言语带有故意伤害。

senses.label: 猛烈有害的
senses.text: 形容循环、竞争或影响不断加剧损害。

usage_groups.group_title: 行为、攻击或话语恶毒残忍
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 带故意伤害或强烈攻击性。

examples.sentence: The debate became personal after a series of vicious remarks.
examples.translation: 一连串恶毒言论之后，这场辩论变成了人身攻击。
examples.teaching_note: vicious remarks 强调言语有攻击性和伤害性。

usage_groups.group_title: 问题形成恶性循环
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 一个坏结果引发另一个坏结果，彼此加剧。
usage_groups.when_to_use: poverty, violence, debt, addiction, politics。

examples.sentence: Poverty and poor education can reinforce each other in a vicious cycle.
examples.translation: 贫困和教育不足会在恶性循环中彼此强化。
examples.teaching_note: vicious cycle 是高频固定表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The punishment was cruel and unnecessary.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这种惩罚残忍且没有必要。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cruel 是常见词；vicious 更带攻击性和恶意。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime used brutal methods to silence dissent.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该政权用残暴手段压制异议。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: brutal 强调暴力或严酷。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rumor was malicious and false.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个谣言恶意且不实。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: malicious 强调意图恶毒，不一定暴力。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her kind response softened the conflict.
antonyms.example_translation: 她友善的回应缓和了冲突。
antonyms.difference_note: kind 与 vicious 的恶毒相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The tumor was benign.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个肿瘤是良性的。
antonyms.difference_note: benign 在医学和抽象语境中表示无害温和。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Greed is often treated as a vice in moral philosophy.
confusable_words.example_translation: 在道德哲学中，贪婪常被视为一种恶习。
confusable_words.difference_note: vice 是名词；vicious 是形容词。

extension.root_affix: vicious 与 vice 有词义联系，围绕恶、坏习性和伤害性展开。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: vicious 已处理多语境，固定表达和派生层分开。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 粗俗的; 昌钝的 lacking sensitivity, refinement, or intelligence: crass remarks 粗俗的话

headline.core_en: showing a lack of sensitivity, refinement, or good judgment

familiar_bridge.summary: crass 不是普通 rude，而是缺乏品味、分寸和敏感度，让人觉得粗俗或愚钝。

core.main: crass 常用于批评言论、玩笑、商业行为或价值观，重点是“不懂分寸、没有细腻判断”。

senses.label: 粗俗无礼；缺乏分寸
senses.text: 形容言行缺乏敏感度、修养或判断力。

usage_groups.group_title: 言论或玩笑粗俗不得体
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说话方式缺乏分寸，让人觉得低级或冒犯。
usage_groups.when_to_use: remarks, jokes, comments, behavior。

examples.sentence: His crass joke ruined the respectful tone of the ceremony.
examples.translation: 他粗俗的玩笑破坏了仪式原本庄重的氛围。
examples.teaching_note: crass joke 强调品味低、不得体。

usage_groups.group_title: 商业或价值观显得粗鄙功利
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把复杂或严肃的东西处理得粗鲁、浅薄或只看钱。

examples.sentence: The memorial was criticized for its crass commercialism.
examples.translation: 这座纪念馆因粗俗的商业化而受到批评。
examples.teaching_note: crass commercialism 批评商业气息破坏了应有的庄重。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His rude reply surprised the guests.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 他粗鲁的回答让客人吃惊。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rude 普通；crass 更强调低级、缺乏分寸。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The advertisement was criticized as vulgar.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这则广告被批评为庸俗。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vulgar 与 crass 接近，常强调低俗品味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His boorish behavior embarrassed the hosts.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他粗鲁无礼的行为让主人难堪。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: boorish 更偏行为举止无礼。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her tactful response avoided an argument.
antonyms.example_translation: 她得体的回应避免了一场争吵。
antonyms.difference_note: tactful 与 crass 的缺乏分寸相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The critic praised the film's refined humor.
antonyms.example_translation: 评论家称赞这部电影幽默优雅。
antonyms.difference_note: refined 强调有品味、有修养。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The comedian used coarse language throughout the show.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这位喜剧演员整场演出都使用粗俗语言。
confusable_words.difference_note: coarse 可指质地粗糙或语言粗俗；crass 更强调判断和敏感度差。

extension.root_affix: 重点记它的语气：粗俗、迟钝、没分寸。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: crass 已避免只译成粗鲁，突出缺乏分寸和品味。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. (态度或做法 ) 务实; 现实; 实事求是 the attitude or practice of accepting a situation as it is and being prepared to deal with it accordingly: The summit was marked by a new mood of realism. 峰会表现出了全新的现实态度 。 n. (描述等 ) 真实;) 写实 the quality or fact of representing a person, thing, or situation accurately or in a way that is true to life: the realism of the characters 对于角色真实的描述 n. (艺术风格 ) 写实; 现实主义 the theory or practice of fidelity in art and literature to nature or to real life and to accurate representation without idealization: the stark realism of the play 戏剧赤裸裸地反映现实近义词 verisimilitude 9 realist /rolist/ n. 现实主义者; 务实的人 a person who accepts a situation as it is and is prepared to deal with it accordingly: J am a realist. 我是个现实主义者 。 i 044] pragmatist

headline.core_cn: 现实主义；务实态度；实事求是
headline.core_en: a practical acceptance of reality, or an artistic style that represents life as it is

familiar_bridge.summary: real 是“真实的”，realism 可以是做人做事的务实，也可以是文学艺术中忠实呈现现实的风格。

core.main: realism 有两个常见方向：一是态度上接受现实条件，二是艺术上呈现现实生活。

senses.label: 务实；现实态度
senses.text: 接受现实条件并据此行动。

senses.label: 现实主义风格
senses.text: 艺术或文学中尽量真实呈现生活。

usage_groups.group_title: 面对现实条件的务实态度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不空谈理想，而是承认限制并处理问题。
usage_groups.when_to_use: politics, diplomacy, policy, planning。

examples.sentence: The negotiators showed realism by accepting that neither side would get everything it wanted.
examples.translation: 谈判者表现出现实态度，承认双方都不可能得到全部想要的东西。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 realism 指务实地面对限制。

usage_groups.group_title: 文学或艺术中的现实主义
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作品不美化现实，而是呈现普通生活和社会矛盾。
usage_groups.when_to_use: fiction, painting, film, theater。

examples.sentence: The novel's realism lies in its attention to ordinary speech and daily labor.
examples.translation: 这部小说的现实主义体现在它对日常语言和劳动的关注。
examples.teaching_note: 艺术义强调真实呈现生活。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Practicality mattered more than elegance in the design.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 在这个设计中，实用性比优雅更重要。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: practicality 偏实际可行；realism 更偏承认现实条件。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The mayor defended the compromise as pragmatism, not weakness.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 市长为这项妥协辩护，称其是务实而非软弱。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pragmatism 强调看效果和可行性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Naturalism in fiction often emphasizes heredity and environment.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 小说中的自然主义常强调遗传和环境。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: naturalism 是文学艺术相关概念，和 realism 有交集但不同。

antonyms.example_sentence: His idealism made compromise difficult.
antonyms.example_translation: 他的理想主义让妥协变得困难。
antonyms.difference_note: idealism 常与务实的 realism 对照。

antonyms.example_sentence: Romanticism valued imagination and emotion over strict realism.
antonyms.example_translation: 浪漫主义重视想象和情感，而不是严格现实主义。
antonyms.difference_note: romanticism 在艺术史中常与 realism 对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The plan failed because it ignored reality.
confusable_words.example_translation: 计划失败了，因为它无视现实。
confusable_words.difference_note: reality 是现实本身；realism 是态度或艺术风格。

extension.root_affix: real 是“真实的”；realism 是围绕真实现实形成的态度或艺术风格。

word_family.note: 常形容计划、目标或描写。

related_words.note: 态度义的重要近义方向。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: realism 已按态度和艺术两义处理，适合 GRE 阅读中的抽象讨论。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 友好的; 平易近人的 friendly, good-natured, or easy to talk to: He is affable to everybody. 他对所有的人都抱友好的态度 。 i 044] easygoing

headline.core_cn: 友善易谈的；和蔼可亲的
headline.core_en: friendly, pleasant, and easy to talk to

familiar_bridge.summary: able 有“能够”的感觉，affable 可以理解成让人容易靠近、容易开口交谈。

core.main: affable 形容人的态度亲切、没有距离感，常用于 host、teacher、leader、manner。

senses.label: 友善易谈；和蔼可亲
senses.text: 形容人态度温和，别人容易和他说话。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或态度亲切、容易接近
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调没有架子，让别人愿意开口。
usage_groups.when_to_use: people, hosts, teachers, leaders, manner。

examples.sentence: The professor was affable enough that first-year students felt comfortable asking questions.
examples.translation: 这位教授很和蔼，新生也敢放心提问。
examples.teaching_note: affable 强调亲切易谈。

usage_groups.group_title: 社交场合中让气氛轻松
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 态度友好，使互动不紧张。
usage_groups.when_to_use: conversation, reception, interview, meeting。

examples.sentence: His affable tone made a difficult negotiation feel less hostile.
examples.translation: 他友善的语气让一场艰难谈判不那么充满敌意。
examples.teaching_note: affable tone 可用于正式互动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The guide was friendly to every visitor.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 导游对每位游客都很友好。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: friendly 很普通；affable 更强调好说话、亲切。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The manager was approachable despite her senior position.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 尽管职位很高，这位经理仍然容易接近。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: approachable 和 affable 很近，强调没有距离感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His genial manner put everyone at ease.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他和蔼愉快的举止让大家放松。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: genial 更强调温暖愉快的气质。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She had an amiable personality and rarely took offense.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 她性格和蔼，很少生气。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: amiable 与 affable 接近，常形容性格温和。

antonyms.example_sentence: The director seemed aloof and rarely spoke to staff.
antonyms.example_translation: 主任显得冷淡疏远，很少和员工说话。
antonyms.difference_note: aloof 与 affable 的亲近感相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness faced hostile questioning.
antonyms.example_translation: 证人遭遇了带敌意的盘问。
antonyms.difference_note: hostile 是更强的反向词。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Her amiable nature made her popular with colleagues.
confusable_words.example_translation: 她和蔼的性格让她很受同事欢迎。
confusable_words.difference_note: amiable 偏性格温和；affable 更偏容易交谈。

extension.root_affix: affable 可重点记作“容易交谈、容易接近”的性格词。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: affable 已以亲切易谈为核心，近义层次清楚。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 加剧; 恶化 make (a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling) worse: exacerbate one's misery 加深自己的不幸 | An anticipated bad harvest will exacerbate social tensions. 预料的坏收成将加剧社会紧张形势 。 i 44] magnify, aggravate

headline.core_en: to make a problem, pain, conflict, or bad situation worse

familiar_bridge.summary: worse 是“更糟”，exacerbate 是正式动词，专门表示让已经不好的情况进一步恶化。

core.main: exacerbate 常接 problem、tension、symptom、crisis 等负面对象，强调火上浇油。

senses.label: 使问题、冲突或症状恶化
senses.text: 让原本不好的情况变得更严重。

usage_groups.group_title: 让问题或局势进一步恶化
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 原本已有问题，某个因素让它更严重。

examples.sentence: The drought exacerbated food shortages across the region.
examples.translation: 干旱加剧了该地区的食物短缺。
examples.teaching_note: exacerbate 常用于负面局势进一步变坏。

usage_groups.group_title: 让疼痛、症状或情绪更严重
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在医学或心理状态。

examples.sentence: Lack of sleep can exacerbate anxiety in many patients.
examples.translation: 睡眠不足会加重许多患者的焦虑。
examples.teaching_note: 医学和心理语境中，exacerbate 很常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new policy worsened the housing shortage.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 新政策让住房短缺更加严重。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: worsen 普通；exacerbate 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Pollution aggravated the child's asthma.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 污染加重了孩子的哮喘。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: aggravate 常用于病情、问题或情绪加重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scandal intensified public anger.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 丑闻加剧了公众愤怒。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: intensify 可中性或负面；exacerbate 几乎总是负面。

antonyms.example_sentence: The medicine alleviated the pain.
antonyms.example_translation: 药物缓解了疼痛。
antonyms.difference_note: alleviate 是减轻痛苦或问题。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new rules may mitigate the risk of fraud.
antonyms.example_translation: 新规定可能降低欺诈风险。
antonyms.difference_note: mitigate 更正式，常用于风险或损害。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The repeated delays exasperated the passengers.
confusable_words.example_translation: 反复延误让乘客恼火。
confusable_words.difference_note: exasperate 是让人生气；exacerbate 是让问题恶化。

extension.root_affix: exacerbate 可和 aggravate 一起记：都表示让负面情况加重。

word_family.note: 名词形式，医学和政策语境常见。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: exacerbate 已准确处理为负面加剧动词，关系区自然。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: #344 v.i. (RSF) 失败; 崩溃 (Of a plan or undertaking) fail or break down, typically as a result of a particular problem or setback: The company foundered during the last recession. 公司在上次经济衰退中倒闭了 。 | The talks foundered. 会谈失败了

headline.core_en: to fail, collapse, or sink after running into serious difficulty

familiar_bridge.summary: founder 作动词不是“创始人”。它常表示计划、谈判或事业因为问题而失败，也可表示船沉没。

core.main: founder 的重点是本来在推进的东西撑不住了，最终失败或沉下去。

senses.label: 计划或事业失败崩溃
senses.text: 因为问题、分歧或挫折而无法继续。

senses.label: 船只沉没
senses.text: 船因为进水或风浪而沉没。

usage_groups.group_title: 计划、谈判或事业因阻碍而失败
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调努力推进到一半却撑不住。

examples.sentence: The peace talks foundered over disagreements about borders.
examples.translation: 和平谈判因边界分歧而破裂。
examples.teaching_note: founder over/on 表示因为某个问题而失败。

usage_groups.group_title: 船只在水中沉没
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 实体义，多见于航海叙述。
usage_groups.when_to_use: ships, boats, storms。

examples.sentence: The ship foundered during the storm, leaving only a few survivors.
examples.translation: 船在暴风雨中沉没，只留下少数幸存者。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项帮助区分 founder 和 flounder。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan failed because funding disappeared.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 计划失败了，因为资金消失了。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fail 普通；founder 更有遇到障碍后崩溃的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The coalition collapsed after months of disagreement.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 联盟在数月分歧后瓦解。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: collapse 更强调结构突然垮掉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The project floundered without clear leadership.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 项目缺乏明确领导，进展艰难。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: flounder 是挣扎不顺；founder 是失败或沉没。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform succeeded because it won broad support.
antonyms.example_translation: 改革成功了，因为它获得广泛支持。
antonyms.difference_note: succeed 与 founder 的失败相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company survived the recession.
antonyms.example_translation: 公司挺过了经济衰退。
antonyms.difference_note: survive 强调没有崩溃。

extension.root_affix: 这里的 founder 要和名词 founder 分开记；动词义围绕失败、崩溃、沉没。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: founder 已按动词义深审，避免误读为创始人。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Gale; 战胜 (困难等 ) overcome (a difficulty or obstacle): surmounting obstacles 战胜困难

headline.core_en: to overcome a difficulty or obstacle

familiar_bridge.summary: mount 有“上升、爬上”的感觉；surmount 就像爬过障碍，最终克服它。

core.main: surmount 常接 obstacle、difficulty、challenge，强调面对阻碍仍然成功越过去。

senses.label: 克服困难或障碍
senses.text: 成功处理或越过阻挡前进的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 克服困难、障碍或挑战
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调成功越过阻力。
usage_groups.when_to_use: obstacles, difficulties, challenges, barriers。

examples.sentence: The researchers surmounted several technical obstacles before the device worked reliably.
examples.translation: 研究人员克服了几项技术障碍，设备才稳定运行。
examples.teaching_note: surmount 强调最终越过障碍。

examples.sentence: The team surmounted repeated setbacks and finished the project.
examples.translation: 团队克服了一次又一次挫折，完成了项目。
examples.teaching_note: surmount 强调困难存在，但最终被克服。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She overcame her fear of public speaking.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 她克服了公开演讲的恐惧。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: overcome 最常用；surmount 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team conquered a problem that had slowed progress for years.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 团队攻克了一个多年拖慢进展的问题。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conquer 更有战胜感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The artist transcended the limits of the genre.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这位艺术家超越了这一类型的限制。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: transcend 强调超越限制，而不只是解决困难。

antonyms.example_sentence: The patient succumbed to the infection.
antonyms.example_translation: 病人不敌感染。
antonyms.difference_note: succumb 表示没能克服。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee yielded to public pressure.
antonyms.example_translation: 委员会向公众压力让步。
antonyms.difference_note: yield 强调让步或屈服。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The problem is difficult but surmountable.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这个问题很难，但可以克服。
confusable_words.difference_note: surmount 是动词；surmountable 是形容词。

extension.root_affix: sur- 有“在上方、越过”的感觉；mount 表示上升。surmount 可理解为“爬过障碍”。

word_family.note: 形容困难并非不可解决。
加 in- 后表示无法越过。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 必要的; 需要的; 必不可少的 needed for a particular purpose: the number of votes requisite for election 当选所需的票数 | Decision is a quality requisite to a commander. 决断是指挥员必须具备的品质

headline.core_en: necessary for a particular purpose, or something required

familiar_bridge.summary: require 是“要求、需要”，requisite 就是某个目标必须具备的条件或要素。

core.main: requisite 可作形容词，表示必要的；也可作名词，表示必备条件。

senses.label: 必要的；必需的
senses.text: 某个目的、职位或结果所必须具备。

senses.label: 必需条件
senses.text: 作为名词，指不可缺少的要素。

usage_groups.group_title: 某事所必需的条件或能力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调没有它就无法完成目标。

examples.sentence: The job requires the requisite technical skills and patience.
examples.translation: 这份工作需要必要的技术能力和耐心。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 requisite 表示完成这份工作所必需的能力。

usage_groups.group_title: 作为名词表示必备条件
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 正式语境中可指某事成功所需的要素。
usage_groups.when_to_use: success, reform, research, leadership。

examples.sentence: Public trust is a requisite for any lasting reform.
examples.translation: 公众信任是任何持久改革的必要条件。
examples.teaching_note: a requisite for 表示某事的必要条件。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Clean water is necessary for health.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 清洁用水对健康是必要的。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: necessary 普通；requisite 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Evidence is essential to a convincing argument.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 证据对有说服力的论证必不可少。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: essential 强调核心必要性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The assistant became indispensable to the project.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这名助理对项目变得不可或缺。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: indispensable 语气更强，表示无法替代。

antonyms.example_sentence: The final essay is optional for extra credit.
antonyms.example_translation: 期末论文是可选加分项。
antonyms.difference_note: optional 与 requisite 的必需相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The extra step was unnecessary and time-consuming.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个额外步骤不必要且耗时。
antonyms.difference_note: unnecessary 是直接反义。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Calculus is a prerequisite for the advanced course.
confusable_words.example_translation: 微积分是这门高级课程的先修条件。
confusable_words.difference_note: prerequisite 是必须先满足的条件；requisite 更泛。

extension.root_affix: requisite 和 require 同方向：都围绕“需要、要求”。prerequisite 是非常重要的迁移词。

word_family.note: pre- 表示在前，指必须先具备的条件。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt. (di; 诱惑; 吸引 to attract (someone) especially by offering or showing something that is appealing, interesting, etc.: They enticed him into joining the conspiracy. 他们诱使他参与阴谋 。 | entice a young girl away from home 诱骗一个姑娘离家出走

headline.core_en: to attract someone by offering something appealing

familiar_bridge.summary: entice 比 attract 更有“用诱人的东西把人引过来”的感觉，常带一点诱惑或策略。

core.main: entice 常用 entice someone to do 或 entice someone into something，表示用好处、机会、奖励吸引人行动。

senses.label: 用诱人条件吸引某人行动
senses.text: 通过利益、承诺、机会或美好画面让人愿意靠近或做某事。

usage_groups.group_title: 用好处或机会吸引某人去做事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 对象通常被某种奖励、折扣、机会吸引。
usage_groups.when_to_use: customers, voters, students, investors。

examples.sentence: The museum offered free admission to entice more families to visit.
examples.translation: 博物馆提供免费入场，以吸引更多家庭参观。

usage_groups.group_title: 诱惑人进入不一定明智的选择
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 有时带负面色彩，像 lure 或 tempt。
usage_groups.when_to_use: risky deals, false promises, unhealthy habits。

examples.sentence: Many investors were enticed by promises of quick profits.
examples.translation: 许多投资者被快速获利的承诺吸引。
examples.teaching_note: be enticed by 常带警惕意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The festival attracted visitors from nearby towns.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个节日吸引了附近城镇的游客。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: attract 普通；entice 更强调用诱因吸引。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The offer lured customers away from competitors.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这项优惠把顾客从竞争对手那里吸引过来。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lure 常带更强诱骗或钓上的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The smell of fresh bread tempted him to enter the bakery.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 新鲜面包的香味诱使他走进面包店。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tempt 更强调欲望被勾起。

antonyms.example_sentence: The harsh design repelled many potential users.
antonyms.example_translation: 粗糙的设计让许多潜在用户反感。
antonyms.difference_note: repel 与 entice 的吸引方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: High fees deterred students from applying.
antonyms.example_translation: 高昂费用让学生不敢申请。
antonyms.difference_note: deter 是让人不去做某事。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She coaxed the child into trying the new food.
confusable_words.example_translation: 她哄孩子尝试新食物。
confusable_words.difference_note: coax 更温和，强调劝说过程。

extension.root_affix: entice 的重点不在词根拆解，而在 entice someone to do / into something 这两个结构。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: entice 已清晰呈现结构和语气，避免只译成吸引。

需要补充 entice to/into 结构

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 促进健康的; 令人振奋的; 使人有活力的 giving a feeling of vigor or well-being: invigorating: the tonic quality of sea ai 海洋空气滋养身心的性质近义词 restorative

headline.core_cn: 使人振奋的；滋补物；补药
headline.core_en: something that restores energy, health, or a feeling of well-being

familiar_bridge.summary: tonic 可以想成让人恢复精神的东西：可能是补药，也可能是一场旅行、一段音乐或一种清新的体验。

core.main: tonic 的核心是恢复活力和精神状态，比 stimulant 更温和，带“滋养、振作”的感觉。

senses.label: 使人恢复活力的；补益的
senses.text: 形容事物让人精神变好、身体或情绪恢复。

senses.label: 补药；滋补物
senses.text: 名词义，指让身体或精神恢复的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人精神振作或恢复活力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在经历、空气、音乐、幽默或休息。

examples.sentence: After months of stress, the quiet week in the mountains was a tonic for her spirits.
examples.translation: 几个月的压力之后，山中安静的一周让她精神恢复了过来。
examples.teaching_note: a tonic for the spirits 表示让人振作的东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 补药或调理身体的东西
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 更接近传统 medicine 或 drink 的用法。
usage_groups.when_to_use: medicine, drink, health, recovery。

examples.sentence: The doctor described the mixture as a mild tonic rather than a cure.
examples.translation: 医生把这种混合物称为温和补剂，而不是治疗药物。
examples.teaching_note: 名词义强调补益而非治愈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The cool air was refreshing after the long climb.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 长途攀登后，凉爽空气令人精神一振。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: refreshing 是熟词；tonic 更正式、更有补益感。

advanced_synonyms.meaning_cn: 令人振奋的；使有活力的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The walk was invigorating after a day indoors.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 在室内待了一天后，这次散步令人精神焕发。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: invigorating 强调重新有活力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Sleep has a restorative effect on memory and mood.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 睡眠对记忆和情绪有恢复作用。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: restorative 更强调恢复。

antonyms.example_sentence: The illness was debilitating for months.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场病让人虚弱了数月。
antonyms.difference_note: debilitating 与 tonic 的恢复活力相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The argument was emotionally draining.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场争论让人情绪上疲惫不堪。
antonyms.difference_note: draining 强调耗尽精力。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Caffeine is a mild stimulant.
confusable_words.example_translation: 咖啡因是一种温和刺激物。
confusable_words.difference_note: stimulant 更强调刺激活跃；tonic 更偏恢复和补益。

extension.root_affix: tonic 不必硬拆词根，重点记住它的语气：让人恢复精神、健康或活力。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: tonic 已覆盖形容词和名词义，语气比 stimulant 更准确。

需要区分 tonic 和 stimulant

反义加入 debilitating/draining

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: Vi， 被解散;被遣散 (of an organized group) break up or cause to break up and stop functioning: At the close of the war the army disbanded. '& 4 4 # Bt F BARGER T «

headline.core_en: to break up an organized group or cause it to stop functioning

familiar_bridge.summary: band 可以是“一群人”，disband 就是让一个组织、队伍或团体散掉，不再作为整体运作。

core.main: disband 的对象通常是有组织的群体，如军队、委员会、乐队、政党或团队。

senses.label: 解散组织或团体
senses.text: 让一个原本有组织的群体停止运作。

usage_groups.group_title: 组织、队伍或委员会停止运作
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调群体作为整体被解散。
usage_groups.when_to_use: army, committee, team, party, band。

examples.sentence: The committee disbanded after issuing its final report.
examples.translation: 委员会发布最终报告后解散。
examples.teaching_note: disbanded 表示组织完成使命后停止运作。

usage_groups.group_title: 官方或外力解散某个团体
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 可表示政府、学校或组织管理者让群体停止活动。

examples.sentence: The government ordered the militia to disband and surrender its weapons.
examples.translation: 政府命令民兵组织解散并交出武器。
examples.teaching_note: order someone to disband 常见于政治或军事语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The band broke up after ten years together.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这个乐队合作十年后解散。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: break up 口语；disband 更正式，强调组织停止运作。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The parliament was dissolved before the election.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 议会在选举前被解散。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dissolve 更正式，常用于机构或法律关系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Police dispersed the crowd after midnight.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 午夜后警方驱散了人群。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disperse 强调人群散开，不一定组织终止。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team assembled in the conference room.
antonyms.example_translation: 团队在会议室集合。
antonyms.difference_note: assemble 是聚集成整体。

antonyms.example_sentence: Students formed a new debating society.
antonyms.example_translation: 学生成立了一个新的辩论社。
antonyms.difference_note: form 与 disband 的解散相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The teacher dismissed the class early.
confusable_words.example_translation: 老师提前下课。
confusable_words.difference_note: dismiss 可指让一群人离开，但不一定让组织永久解散。

extension.root_affix: dis- 表示分开；band 表示一组人。disband 就是让一群人分散、不再成团。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: disband 已自然呈现群体解散意象，没有把普通搭配放入派生层。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 大范围的; 影响广泛的 wide in range or effect: sweeping reforms KT FHKE

headline.core_cn: 广泛的；大范围的；笼统的
headline.core_en: wide in range or effect, sometimes too general

familiar_bridge.summary: sweep 是“扫过”，sweeping 像一口气扫过大片范围，所以表示影响广泛；也可指说法太笼统。

core.main: sweeping 一方面常赞成改革影响大，另一方面也可批评判断太宽泛、没分寸。

senses.label: 影响范围广泛的
senses.text: 形容改革、变化或权力覆盖面很大。

senses.label: 过于笼统的
senses.text: 形容判断、声明或概括太宽，缺少细节。

usage_groups.group_title: 改革、变化或权力影响范围很广
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调覆盖面大，影响许多领域或人群。

examples.sentence: The new law introduced sweeping reforms to the education system.
examples.translation: 新法律对教育体系进行了大范围改革。
examples.teaching_note: sweeping reforms 是高频搭配，强调影响广泛。

usage_groups.group_title: 判断或概括过于笼统
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 带批评意味，认为说法覆盖太宽。
usage_groups.when_to_use: claims, statements, generalizations, assumptions。

examples.sentence: The critic made a sweeping generalization about modern poetry.
examples.translation: 这位评论家对现代诗作出了过于笼统的概括。
examples.teaching_note: sweeping generalization 常带负面评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy had broad support among voters.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这项政策获得选民广泛支持。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: broad 普通；sweeping 更强调范围大或一口气覆盖很多。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report offers a wide-ranging analysis of migration.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 报告对移民问题进行了广泛分析。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wide-ranging 比 sweeping 更中性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee called for a comprehensive review.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 委员会要求进行全面审查。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: comprehensive 强调覆盖完整。

antonyms.example_sentence: The change had only a limited effect.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个变化影响有限。
antonyms.difference_note: limited 与 sweeping 的广泛相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The study took a narrow view of the issue.
antonyms.example_translation: 这项研究对问题采取了狭窄视角。
antonyms.difference_note: narrow 可用于视角或范围。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The storm swept across the coast.
confusable_words.example_translation: 风暴席卷海岸。
confusable_words.difference_note: swept 是 sweep 的过去式；sweeping 作形容词表示广泛或笼统。

extension.root_affix: sweep 是扫过；sweeping 把这个画面延伸成“扫过很大范围”。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: sweeping 已覆盖正向大范围和负向笼统两类语境。

需要补充 sweeping generalization 负面义

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 充满的; 装满的; 充斥的 filled or well-supplied with something: a trip replete with unexpected thrills 一次充满意想不到的刺激经历的旅行 | a home replete with every modern convenience 现代化设备一应俱全的家庭 put sb./sth. to the test phr. 使受到考验; 使经受检验 find out how useful, strong, or effective someone or something is: A trip through the desert will put the truck to the test. 穿越沙漠将使这俩卡车受到考验

headline.core_en: filled or abundantly supplied with something

familiar_bridge.summary: replete 比 full 更书面，常和 with 连用，表示某物充满了某种内容、细节、问题或机会。

core.main: replete with 是常见结构；它常用于书面语描述文本、经历、历史、系统或场景。

senses.label: 充满；满是
senses.text: 常接 with，表示某物包含大量某种东西。

usage_groups.group_title: 某物充满细节、例子或特征
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调内容丰富或大量存在。
usage_groups.when_to_use: books, reports, histories, landscapes, experiences。

examples.sentence: The book is replete with examples drawn from everyday life.
examples.translation: 这本书充满了来自日常生活的例子。
examples.teaching_note: replete with 是固定核心结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 充斥问题、矛盾或危险
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不一定是正面，也可以满是不良因素。

examples.sentence: The proposal was replete with contradictions that the committee could not ignore.
examples.translation: 这项提案充满矛盾，委员会无法忽视。
examples.teaching_note: replete 可用于负面内容大量存在。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room was full of smoke.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 房间里满是烟。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: full 普通；replete 更书面，常接 with。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The archive is filled with letters from the period.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 档案馆里满是那个时期的信件。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: filled 更普通直接。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The organization was rife with corruption.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这个组织腐败盛行。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rife 通常用于负面事物大量存在。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speech was devoid of evidence.
antonyms.example_translation: 这场演讲完全没有证据。
antonyms.difference_note: devoid of 与 replete with 结构相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plan was lacking in detail.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个计划缺乏细节。
antonyms.difference_note: lacking 表示不足。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The report is complete and ready to submit.
confusable_words.example_translation: 报告完整，可以提交。
confusable_words.difference_note: complete 是完整；replete 是充满某物。

extension.root_affix: replete 的核心结构是 replete with；和 complete 形近但语义不同。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 切题的;紧密相关的 relevant to a subject under consideration: Your remark is not germane to the discussion. 你的话与这次讨论无甚关系 。 if 44] relevant

headline.core_en: relevant to the matter being discussed

familiar_bridge.summary: relevant 是“相关的”，germane 更书面，常用来判断某个观点、证据或问题是否切中当前主题。

core.main: germane 常和 to 连用，表示内容和讨论主题真正有关，而不是跑题或外在。

senses.label: 切题的；相关的
senses.text: 对当前讨论、论证或问题有直接关系。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点、证据或问题切合当前主题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调不是随便相关，而是对当前讨论有用。

examples.sentence: Her objection was germane to the debate because it challenged the central assumption.
examples.translation: 她的反对意见切中这场辩论，因为它挑战了核心假设。
examples.teaching_note: germane to 常用于论证和讨论。

usage_groups.group_title: 排除不相关信息时强调切题性
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在学术写作或正式讨论。
usage_groups.when_to_use: legal reasoning, academic analysis, policy debate。

examples.sentence: The judge ruled that the evidence was not germane to the case.
examples.translation: 法官裁定这项证据与本案无关。
examples.teaching_note: not germane to 是正式表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence is relevant to the question.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这项证据与这个问题相关。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: relevant 普通；germane 更正式、更强调切题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The student asked a pertinent question about the method.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 学生提出了一个与方法相关且切题的问题。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pertinent 与 germane 很近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic made an apposite comparison between the two novels.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 评论家对两部小说作出了贴切比较。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: apposite 更强调恰当、贴切。

antonyms.example_sentence: The comment was interesting but irrelevant to the main issue.
antonyms.example_translation: 这条评论有趣，但与核心问题无关。
antonyms.difference_note: irrelevant 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The editor removed extraneous details from the essay.
antonyms.example_translation: 编辑删去了文章中无关的细节。
antonyms.difference_note: extraneous 强调外在、不属于主题。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The article was translated from German.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这篇文章由德语翻译而来。
confusable_words.difference_note: German 形近但完全不同。

extension.root_affix: germane 重点记结构 germane to，表示与当前主题切实相关。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. [a]; 视 … 为相同 consider (one thing) to be the same as or equivalent to another: equate wealth with success 把财富和成功等同起来

headline.core_en: to regard one thing as the same as, or equivalent to, another

familiar_bridge.summary: equal 是“相等”，equate 是动词：把两个东西看成相等或等同。

core.main: equate 常用于思想和价值判断：把成功等同于财富，把不同概念看成同一回事。

senses.label: 把两个事物等同起来
senses.text: 认为 A 和 B 意义、价值或作用相同。

usage_groups.group_title: 把某种价值或概念等同于另一种
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在批评过度简化的判断。

examples.sentence: The essay warns against equating economic growth with human well-being.
examples.translation: 这篇文章提醒人们不要把经济增长等同于人的福祉。
examples.teaching_note: equate A with B 常用于抽象论证。

usage_groups.group_title: 在数学或分析中视为相等
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 更接近 equation 的逻辑。
usage_groups.when_to_use: quantities, variables, values, measures。

examples.sentence: The model equates higher demand with higher prices.
examples.translation: 这个模型把更高需求与更高价格对应起来。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 equate 更偏分析关系中的等同。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The study compares two approaches to teaching reading.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这项研究比较了两种阅读教学方法。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: compare 是比较；equate 是认为相等或等同。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Some critics identify modernity with constant change.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 一些评论者把现代性等同于持续变化。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: identify with 在学术写作中可表示等同。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article conflates poverty with laziness.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这篇文章将贫困和懒惰混为一谈。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conflate 常带负面，表示不该混同却混同。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author distinguishes evidence from speculation.
antonyms.example_translation: 作者区分了证据和猜测。
antonyms.difference_note: distinguish 与 equate 的等同相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The test differentiates advanced learners from beginners.
antonyms.example_translation: 这项测试区分高级学习者和初学者。
antonyms.difference_note: differentiate 更正式。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Two plus two equals four.
confusable_words.example_translation: 二加二等于四。
confusable_words.difference_note: equal 可作动词或形容词；equate 更强调人为地把两者等同起来。

extension.root_affix: equ- 与“相等”有关；equate、equal、equation、equivalent 都在同一组。

word_family.note: 数学和抽象分析中常见。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: equate 已从 equal 建立熟悉感，并清楚区分 compare/conflate。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (表达观点时 ) 专横的; 教条的; 不容他人质疑的 inclined to lay down principles as incontrovertibly true: a dogmatic supporter of apartheid 种族隔离的专横支持者 | You cannot be dogmatic with truth. 对于真理你不能武断

headline.core_cn: 教条的；武断的；不容质疑的
headline.core_en: asserting beliefs as unquestionably true, often without allowing debate

familiar_bridge.summary: dogma 是“教条”，dogmatic 形容人或观点把自己的原则当成绝对真理，不愿被质疑。

core.main: dogmatic 的问题不只是坚定，而是过度自信、拒绝讨论和证据修正。

senses.label: 教条武断；不容质疑
senses.text: 形容观点或态度把某些原则当作绝对真理。

usage_groups.group_title: 观点或语气不容讨论
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调说话者把自己的判断当成定论。

examples.sentence: The professor's dogmatic tone discouraged students from asking questions.
examples.translation: 这位教授武断的语气让学生不敢提问。
examples.teaching_note: dogmatic tone 强调不容反驳。

usage_groups.group_title: 制度或思想体系僵化守旧
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 常批评理论、宗教或政治立场拒绝变化。
usage_groups.when_to_use: doctrine, ideology, policy, tradition。

examples.sentence: A dogmatic approach to policy leaves little room for evidence or compromise.
examples.translation: 教条式政策方法几乎不给证据和妥协留下空间。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调僵化地坚持原则。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rules were too rigid to fit local conditions.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这些规则太死板，无法适应当地情况。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rigid 可形容规则或思维；dogmatic 更偏观点不容质疑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform failed because its leaders were doctrinaire.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 改革失败了，因为领导者过于教条。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: doctrinaire 与 dogmatic 很近，更政治或理论化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He was opinionated but not always well informed.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 他固执己见，但并不总是见识充分。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: opinionated 偏个人固执，dogmatic 更像原则化地武断。

antonyms.example_sentence: An open-minded scientist changes views when evidence changes.
antonyms.example_translation: 思想开放的科学家会随证据改变观点。
antonyms.difference_note: open-minded 与 dogmatic 的不容质疑相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy was flexible enough to adapt to new data.
antonyms.example_translation: 这项政策足够灵活，能适应新数据。
antonyms.difference_note: flexible 强调可调整。

confusable_words.example_sentence: An assertive speaker can be clear without being dogmatic.
confusable_words.example_translation: 坚定自信的演讲者可以表达清楚而不武断。
confusable_words.difference_note: assertive 不一定拒绝讨论；dogmatic 往往拒绝质疑。

extension.root_affix: dogma 是教条；dogmatic 是把教条当成绝对真理的态度。

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: dogmatic 已自然连接 dogma，并突出不容质疑的核心。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 权威的说法; 教条 a principle or set of principles laid down by an authority as incontrovertibly true: These new findings challenge the current dogma in the field. 这些新发现挑战了这一领域的权威观点

headline.core_en: a belief or set of beliefs accepted as unquestionably true by an authority or group

familiar_bridge.summary: dogma 是某个团体、宗教或意识形态要求人接受的“真理”，通常不鼓励质疑。

core.main: dogma 常出现在宗教、政治、理论和学术范式中，重点是“被权威固定下来的信条”。

senses.label: 教条；权威信条
senses.text: 被某个权威或群体当作不可质疑的原则。

usage_groups.group_title: 宗教、政治或理论体系中的信条
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调原则被固定下来并要求接受。
usage_groups.when_to_use: religion, ideology, doctrine, theory。

examples.sentence: The movement treated its leader's ideas as dogma rather than as claims to be tested.
examples.translation: 这个运动把领导者的想法当作教条，而不是可以检验的主张。
examples.teaching_note: dogma 强调不可质疑的权威性。

usage_groups.group_title: 挑战既有权威观点
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多出现在批评某领域把旧观念当定论。
usage_groups.when_to_use: science, education, criticism, reform。

examples.sentence: The discovery challenged the dogma that the disease was purely genetic.
examples.translation: 这一发现挑战了该疾病完全由遗传决定的教条。
examples.teaching_note: challenge dogma 是学术和科学史中常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The belief shaped the community's daily practices.
basic_synonyms.example_translation: 这一信念塑造了社区的日常做法。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: belief 很泛；dogma 是被权威固定并要求接受的信念。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The doctrine influenced legal decisions for decades.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 这一学说影响了数十年的法律判决。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: doctrine 可中性；dogma 往往带僵化意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The group's creed emphasized sacrifice and obedience.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该团体的信条强调牺牲和服从。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: creed 常用于宗教或价值体系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory challenged the scientific orthodoxy of its time.
advanced_synonyms.example_translation: 该理论挑战了当时的科学正统。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: orthodoxy 指主流正统观点。

antonyms.example_sentence: Scientific skepticism requires evidence before belief.
antonyms.example_translation: 科学怀疑精神要求先有证据再相信。
antonyms.difference_note: skepticism 与盲目接受 dogma 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The idea was once treated as heresy by the establishment.
antonyms.example_translation: 这个观点曾被权威机构视为异端。
antonyms.difference_note: heresy 是对正统教条的挑战。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The doctrine became part of official policy.
confusable_words.example_translation: 这一学说成为官方政策的一部分。
confusable_words.difference_note: doctrine 不一定负面；dogma 常暗示僵化或不可质疑。

extension.root_affix: dogma 是基础名词；dogmatic、dogmatism、dogmatist 都从它展开。

related_words.note: 常和 dogma 一起出现在思想史讨论中。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: dogma 已从权威信条和挑战教条两个语境展开。
